headline_en: Frontier AI discourse centered on agentic model releases, evaluation discipline, and the hardware-software economics that make new capabilities practical.

AI infrastructure was the throughline: agent platforms split orchestration from execution, coding tools tightened controls and usage accounting, and industry voices debated whether open-weight models and domestic compute stacks will reshape where value accrues.

Agent containment, AI compute buildout, and agent-driven workflows dominated the day, with builders focusing on practical security boundaries, changing product roles, and the economics of scaling intelligence.
